raw_consumer_statement:
We purchase from XXXX often. Often enough we get defective products. To protect ourselves we use a credit card when paying because this gives us access to the credit card companies chargeback policy which is a better protection than XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. Unfortunately we recently purchased around XXXX  bags and the vast majority are leaking from a defective seal in the seam of the bag. Out of an hour and a half of testing we found 2 bags that didn't leak and the rest leaked. This is after going through about XXXX  during packaging trying to find some that didn't leak. 

Despite this the seller ( on XXXX, basically wholesale XXXX ) keeps telling Amex that there is a policy that prohibits chargebacks, and no matter how many times XXXX  or myself tells Amex there is no such policy they keep siding with the seller. 

Further they claim there is no proof of that this is defective but I've uploaded over an hour and a half of video opening boxes, filling bags with water, and showing they leak out of the side of the bag. 

They then claim I've refused a feasible solution, but the only solution offered is for the seller to send me about XXXX bags to replace the XXXX bags and the kicker on top of this is these XXXX bags are from the same run where the other XXXX bags leak .... So they will be leaking too... 

It is honestly like my chargeback is getting auto denied over and over again for no good reason. 

I am just trying to get someone to give this an honest review. I am happy to do whatever is needed, record more videos of the bags leaking, test the videos on a live chat, and show as much as I can, but they are just auto denying it. 


XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX If the Buyer selects credit card as the method of payment, the Buyer shall be entitled to request for chargeback. In such event, the Seller shall cooperate and bear the burden of proof. In the event where the Seller does not provide any evidence or fails to prove its case, the Seller shall be liable for the relevant losses. Whether the Seller is able to successfully prove its case will usually be based on the relevant credit card issuers determination. ''
